steps.title: Spot the assumption Douglass contradicts
steps.content: Technology investors and enthusiasts assume interactive media is modern and will displace books. Douglass directly rebuts this by showing that the concept of “interactive” text is much older than they assume—it extends back to when readers scratched notes in margins.

steps.title: Match that function to the correct answer
steps.content: Choice A captures this function by naming it as a challenge to the investors' stance. The other three mistake the passage's purpose: B treats the underlined portion as supporting the investors' claim rather than attacking it, C invents a comparison never made in the text, and D mischaracterizes a simple historical point as being about technological challenges. That matches choice A — A is correct.
